prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review | Statement: ADF/TRE/TWF reduced HOMA-IR by MD -0.35 vs non-intervention diet (8 studies, I-squared 0%, GRADE moderate).
2. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review | Statement: TRE reduced triacylglycerols by MD -18.19 mg/dL vs unrestricted diet (9 studies, I-squared 97.2%).
3. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review | Statement: In patients with metabolic syndrome, TWF/MADF improved 13 metabolic and anthropometric outcomes vs CER/RD across 4-6 trials.
4. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting and health outcomes: an umbrella review | Statement: Zeng 2022 evaluated TWF/MADF/TRE/IF in 6 metabolic syndrome trials (n=351, 8-16 weeks) vs non-intervention diet.
5. [SILVER] Source: The metabolic effects of intermittent versus continuous feed | Statement: Intermittent (bolus) feeding regimens in critically ill patients have been of increasing clinician and scientific interest.
